sections.title: Stained Glass
sections: A collection of stained glass pieces built to last. Each design is planned, shaped, and assembled with both structure and visual impact in mind.

items.title: School of Shaolin Kung Fu
about: This piece was created as a stained glass adaptation of the School of Shaolin Kung Fu’s logo. The goal was not to redesign, but to accurately represent it in glass.
Focus was placed on structure, color balance, and clean assembly to ensure the design translates well from a flat graphic into a durable physical piece.
Each component was shaped and fitted to maintain the integrity of the original logo while working within the constraints and strengths of stained glass construction.
description: This stained glass piece was created for the School of Shaolin Kung Fu, based on their established logo and visual identity. The design focuses on accurately translating the original artwork into glass while maintaining clarity, balance, and durability.

Layered colors, defined shapes, and strong contrast were used to ensure the design remains clear at a distance while still holding detail up close. Each section was cut, fitted, and assembled to preserve the structure of the original logo in a glass format.
The result is a clean, durable piece that reflects the school’s identity while adapting it into a physical, light-driven medium.

details.Medium: Stained glass with lead came construction
details.Primary Symbols:
Dragon (left) – internal strength, wisdom
Tiger (right) – external power, courage
Crossed weapons – discipline and martial training
Yin-yang – balance and duality
details.Color Palette:
Green (dragon) – growth, control
Orange/black (tiger) – power, intensity
Red accents – energy, tradition
Style: Traditional martial arts symbolism with decorative glasswork
